text:
REGIME — Defensive rotation dominated; financials and utilities led, tech and industrials lagged.

INDICES — VIX 30.6, elevated but falling. DIA +0.15% led, SPY -0.33%, QQQ -0.76%, IWM -1.44% worst; large caps outperformed small caps decisively. USO surged +4.53%, the standout cross-asset move on the day.

SECTORS — XLF +1.15%, XLC +0.86%, XLU +0.72%, XLRE +0.50%, XLV +0.39%, XLB +0.37%, XLP +0.12%, XLY -0.02%, XLE -0.96%, XLI -1.63%, XLK -1.86%. Top industries KIE +1.61% and WCLD +1.36%; bottom MSOS -3.96% and WGMI -8.13%.

THE 7s — Software +2.76%, Toll Road +2.05%, Insurance +1.76%, Health +1.09%, Old Staples +1.08%, New Staples +0.39%, Bank +0.12%, Freight +0.00%, Power -0.16%, Cloud -0.17%, Housing -0.41%, Old Metals -0.82%, Energy -0.96%, Medtech -1.47%, Defense -1.70%, Industrial -2.21%, Semi -2.97%, New Metals -3.14%, Buildout -4.22%, Fab 7 -5.30%. Strongest ticker in Software 7 not specified; weakest in Fab 7 not individually named in data.

SIGNALS — Fear & Greed 46.8, Neutral. Top movers: BSX -9.02% (Medtech, 3.1x vol spike), Buildout -4.22% (baskets), WGMI -8.13% (industries). 20d momentum: best sector XLE +9.34%, worst XLI -12.21%; best basket Energy +11.23%, worst Housing -13.84%. No confirmed catalyst available for today's specific moves.

WATCH — BSX: -9.02%, 3.1x volume spike warrants review of any earnings or guidance revision. WGMI: -8.13% today, crypto miners showing accelerating breakdown worth monitoring for follow-through. USO: +4.53% while XLE -0.96%, a rare divergence between oil futures and energy equities signaling potential catch-up or reversal.
